perturbation.original_text: 1.03. Terms. Terms are net cash upon delivery.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.03. Terms. Terms are to be determined on a per-order basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract specifies 'net cash upon delivery'. Changing this to 'Terms are to be determined on a per-order basis' introduces ambiguity. This creates an in-text contradiction in Article V Section 5.01 where there is a reference to 'Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03', as this section no longer definitively defines the terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.01. Products Warranty. Company warrants that Distributor shall acquire Products purchased hereunder free and clear of all liens and encumbrances except for Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03, above. Company further warrants all Products to be free from defects in material or workmanship under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of delivery. Any defects must be replaced by the Company within sixty (60) days within this scope of the warranty and all charges for labor and material, will be borne by Company. If it is determined that either no fault exists in Company, or the damage was caused by negligence of Distributor, its agents, employees or customers, Distributor agrees to pay all charges associated with each such replacement. THIS CONSTITUTES THE SOLE WARRANTY MADE BY COMPANY EITHER EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED. THERE ARE NO OTHER WARRANTIES EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED WHICH EXTEND BEYOND THE FACE HEREOF, HEREIN, INCLUDING THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES AND DISTRIBUTOR'S REMEDIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF NONCONFORMING UNITS OR PARTS.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 'net cash upon delivery' for terms, providing a clear definition. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating 'Terms are to be determined on a per-order basis.' This contradicts Article V Section 5.01, which references 'Company's purchase money security interest defined in Section 1.03'. Since Section 1.03 no longer definitively defines the terms, the security interest definition becomes unclear, leading to a contradiction. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language defining payment terms is removed creating a contradiction with obligations in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The distribution right shall be limited to all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America ("Territory") (Exhibit "B" attached hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: The distribution right shall be limited to certain countries within the continent known as South America ("Territory"). Refer to Exhibit "B" for a detailed description.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of 'Territory' to 'certain countries' but not clearly delineating which countries within the South American continent are included creates ambiguity. The original text specifies all countries, while the modified text restricts it. This contradicts Exhibit B, if Exhibit B is not modified or updated, by making the distribution right vague, and creating an in-text contradiction in Exhibit B. It is worth noting that Exhibit B is referring to Section 1.01 and the contradiction is taking place withing that paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT B DESCRIPTION OF THE TERRITORY The Territory which is subject to Section 1.01 of this Agreement includes all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Territory' from 'all countries' to 'certain countries' within South America. This directly contradicts Exhibit B, which explicitly states that the Territory includes 'all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America.' The change introduces ambiguity and a direct inconsistency, making the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable regarding the scope of distribution rights, which aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: 4.01 Patent Infringement. Company agrees, at its own expense, to indemnify, defend and hold harmless each Distributor and its customers from and against every expense, damage, cost and loss (including attorneys' fees incurred) and to satisfy all judgments and decrees resulting from a claim, suit or proceeding insofar as it is based upon an allegation that any Product or any part thereof furnished by Company or any process which is practiced in the customary use of the Product is or has been infringing upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right, if Company is notified promptly of such claim in writing and given authority, and full and proper information and assistance (at Company's expense) for the defense of same. In case any Product, or any part thereof, if such suit is held to constitute an infringement and the use of said Product or part is enjoined, Company shall, in its sole discretion and at its own expense, either procure for the indemnitee the right to continue using said Product or provide or procure for the Distributor a similar product.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.01 Patent Infringement. Company agrees to indemnify Distributor from judgments resulting from a claim that any Product infringes upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right, if Company is notified promptly of such claim in writing and given authority and assistance for the defense of same. In case any Product, if such suit is held to constitute an infringement and the use of said Product or part is enjoined, Company shall, in its sole discretion, either procure for the indemnitee the right to continue using said Product or provide or procure for the Distributor a similar product.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states 'at its own expense' regarding indemnification, defense, and holding harmless the Distributor and its customers, and further including attorneys' fees incurred. The modified text removes the 'at its own expense' for the initial indemnification, removes explicit coverage for 'customers', and omits coverage for every expense, damage, cost and loss (including attorneys' fees incurred), creating an in-text contradiction. Even though 'at its own expense' remains at the end, this implies the company would only incur costs on its sole discretion when procuring the right to use the Product or providing a similar product, creating a contradiction regarding who pays legal expenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.01 Patent Infringement. Company agrees to indemnify Distributor from judgments resulting from a claim that any Product infringes upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right, if Company is notified promptly of such claim in writing and given authority and assistance for the defense of same. In case any Product, if such suit is held to constitute an infringement and the use of said Product or part is enjoined, Company shall, in its sole discretion, either procure for the indemnitee the right to continue using said Product or provide or procure for the Distributor a similar product.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes 'at its own expense' regarding indemnification and omits coverage for 'every expense, damage, cost and loss (including attorneys' fees incurred)', creating a contradiction. While 'at its own expense' remains at the end, it now implies the company only incurs costs when procuring the right to use the Product or providing a similar product. This contradicts the implicit agreement in the contradicted text of the Company being responsible for all expenses, damages, costs, and losses including attorney's fees.
